entries.desc:
Coercer Coerce
Coercer is a powerful authentication coercion tool that automates the abuse of 17 methods in 5 RPC protocols. To coerce HTTP Auth we must use Coercer-1.6
The scan mode tests the RPC calls listening on a victim machine to determine if we can abuse them to coerce authentication. |
entries.heading: Credential Theft > Authentication Coercion > Coercer (Coerce SMB & HTTP [w/ v1.6])

entries.desc:
HTTP NTLM Authentication Coercion (only release 1.6 successfully coerces HTTP NTLM authentication successfully )
The scan mode tests the RPC calls listening on a victim machine to determine if we can abuse them to coerce authentication. |
The coerce mode abuses the RPC calls on a victim machine to coerce authentication so that we can relay them over to relay targets, using ntlmrelayx |
entries.heading: Credential Theft > Authentication Coercion > Coercer (Coerce SMB & HTTP [w/ v1.6])

entries.cmd: ticketer.py -nthash d172f136edc2366d2aa411e92ab6ca01 -domain-sid S-1-5-21-1207890233-375443991-2397730614 -domain inlanefreight.local -spn cifs/ws01.inlanefreight.local Administrator
entries.desc:
Use ticketer.py to Forge a Silver Ticket as Administrator
relaying a DC's NTLM authentication requires specifying the DomainController template
Preventing RCE with machine account is Windows security feature - machine accounts are restricted from performing remote administration tasks, even on themselves. That's why the rpc_s_access_denied error appears with smbexec if you tried to use this hash directly. Instead we need to create a silver ticket. |
entries.heading: Advanced NTLM Relay Attacks > Advanced NTLM Relay Attacks Targeting AD CS > Attacking With NTLMRelayx

Preventing RCE with machine account is Windows security feature - machine accounts are restricted from performing remote administration tasks, even on themselves. That's why the rpc_s_access_denied error appears with smbexec if you tried to use this hash directly. Instead we need to create a silver ticket. |
